General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 26, 2026

Departing Geneva toward the Matterhorn.

Alpine terrain operations require specialized approach planning due to severe wind shear and terrain-induced turbulence around high-altitude peaks like the Matterhorn.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

“Please Do Something”: Pilots Have Complained About LaGuardia Airport’s Safety For Years

Pilots at LaGuardia Airport have reported multiple near-miss situations due to unclear instructions from air traffic control.

Chronic ATC communication failures at LaGuardia create systemic accident risk that regulatory oversight has failed to address despite years of pilot safety reports.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

House Committee Advances ALERT Act

Measure includes collision-mitigation requirements, changes for military aircraft operations and limits on some ADS-B data use.

The legislation balances safety modernization with operational flexibility by coupling mandatory collision-avoidance tech to military carve-outs and privacy protections for ADS-B transparency data.

See AVweb →

International Mar 26, 2026

China sends radar mapping and GNSS augmentation sats into orbit with pair of launches

China conducted a pair of launches in recent days, adding new satellites to the SuperView and CentiSpace low Earth orbit infrastructure constellations.

China's expanded satellite infrastructure enhances positioning accuracy and Earth observation capabilities, strengthening its competitive positioning in global navigation and mapping services critical to commercial aviation operations.

See SpaceNews →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

How to Assess Safety Risk

A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.

Part 5 compliance requires systematic hazard identification and mitigation documentation, making formal risk assessment frameworks essential for operators seeking regulatory approval.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

This Airline Has The World’s Most Comfortable Premium Economy Seat In 2026

The Dubai-based carrier's premium economy product is relatively new.

Emirates' newly refined premium economy positioning demonstrates how legacy carriers are competing for mid-tier passengers against growing ultra-long-haul competition.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Arctic Winter Sea Ice Ties Record Low, NASA, NSIDC Scientists Find

For the second consecutive year, winter sea ice in the Arctic reached a level that matches the lowest peak observed since satellite monitoring began in 1979. On March 15, Arctic sea ice extent reached 5.52 million square miles (14.29 million square kilometers), very close to the 2025 peak of 5.53...

Declining Arctic sea ice reduces reflectivity and increases heat absorption, affecting atmospheric circulation patterns that influence polar jet streams and aviation weather routing efficiency.

See NASA →

Military Mar 26, 2026

5 Structural Advantages Of The A-10 Warthog For Close Air Support Missions

This aircraft offers some impressive offensive and defensive capabilities.

The A-10's redundant systems and damage tolerance design allow it to sustain combat injuries that would ground most aircraft, making it uniquely suited for low-altitude threat environments.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Authorities Searching For American Airlines Flight Attendant Reported Missing In Colombia

The disappearance of an American Airlines flight attendant in Medellin, Colombia, has left his family desperate for answers.

Flight crew safety vulnerabilities extend beyond the aircraft to ground operations in high-risk regions, requiring enhanced security protocols for layovers in areas with documented security concerns.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they happen to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before incidents occur.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s IXPE Gets Fresh Look at Supernova

NASA’s IXPE (Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer) mission has taken a new observation of a supernova, RCW 86, seen here in an image released on March 24, 2026. This observation helps fill in a fuller picture of what other telescopes have seen. The full image combines IXPE’s data with legacy...

IXPE's X-ray polarimetry data complements traditional telescope observations to reveal previously undetectable magnetic field structures in supernova remnants, advancing high-energy astrophysics research.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

Colorado’s Rocky Mountain Metro Airport Launches Unleaded Avgas

UL94 sales begin with state-backed price support and STC reimbursement.

State-subsidized pricing and STC reimbursement programs can accelerate unleaded fuel adoption at regional airports, creating a template for overcoming conversion barriers elsewhere.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Satlantis earnings grow alongside demand for Earth-observation satellites

WASHINGTON – Satlantis reported revenues of 47.8 million euros ($56.4 million) in 2025, with more than 50% of the Spanish company’s income derived from small-satellite sales and operations. Audited earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization were 14.4 million euros. The results...

Rising profitability in small satellite manufacturing signals expanding commercial opportunities for Earth-observation data services and applications.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Only 54% Full: Delta Air Lines' 10 Emptiest Long-Haul Routes Revealed

How many of them can you guess? There are some intriguing surprises.

Delta's persistently half-empty long-haul routes signal potential capacity reallocation opportunities as carriers reassess international demand patterns post-pandemic.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

KSQL Noise Abatement Departure

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures reflect growing community pressure on regional airports to balance operational efficiency with residential constraints in congested metropolitan airspace.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

HTS Market Set to Reach $76B as Industry Enters Terabit Era

Paris, France – March, 2026 – Novaspace’s latest High Throughput Satellites (HTS) report shows global demand reaching 218 Tbps by 2034, while service revenues are set to more than double to $76 billion over the same period. The findings […]...

Satellite operators must prepare infrastructure and partnerships now to capture market share in a market doubling to $76B as terabit-scale capacity becomes standard by 2034.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Brazil Rolls Out First Supersonic Fighter Built in-Country

A Saab-Embraer program leads to first supersonic fighter assembled in Brazil.

Brazil's domestic fighter assembly capability reduces dependency on foreign suppliers and establishes competitive advantage in Latin American defense markets.

See AVweb →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Dreamliner groundings push Kenya Airways back into the red

Kenya Airways returned to financial losses in 2025, the airline reported on March 24, 2026. The Kenyan flag…...

Boeing's 787 delivery delays and in-service groundings are creating cascading financial damage for African carriers whose narrow fleets lack redundancy to absorb aircraft unavailability.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

AMA Advocacy: H.R. 6460 passes U.S. House of Representatives

On Tuesday, March 24, H.R. 6460, a bill championed by AMA that will simplify altitude...

Simplified altitude restrictions for recreational drone operations could accelerate hobbyist adoption and reduce regulatory friction for the broader UAS ecosystem.

See sUAS News →

General Aviation Mar 26, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Regional airports like Cedar Rapids serve as critical training hubs where flight schools develop commercial pilots while simultaneously accommodating air carrier operations, creating integrated ecosystems that benefit both airline workforce development and community connectivity.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Curiosity Blog, Sols 4838-4844: Wrapping Up the Boxwork Terrain

Written by Deborah Padgett, MSL Operations Product Ground System Task Lead at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory Earth planning date: Friday, March 20, 2026 Curiosity has just concluded a very intense week of science observations and engineering activities, as it wraps up its monthslong investigation...

Curiosity's extended boxwork terrain study demonstrates how sustained rover operations optimize scientific efficiency through integrated engineering and science workflow management.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

FAA Presses California for Records in Airport-Funding Dispute

California airport funding dispute draws new FAA scrutiny.

FAA's records request signals potential enforcement action beyond typical funding disputes, suggesting possible violations of federal airport grant conditions or misuse of federal infrastructure dollars.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

La NASA anuncia la cobertura de la de la primera misión tripulada Artemis alrededor de la Luna

Diversos eventos previos al lanzamiento, del lanzamiento y de la misión Artemis II de la NASA alrededor de la Luna se transmitirán en línea. La agencia tiene como fecha objetivo no antes del miércoles 1 de abril para este vuelo de prueba, con una ventana de lanzamiento de dos horas que se abre a...

NASA's Artemis II lunar mission coverage will stream online with April 1 as the target launch date, allowing real-time global audience engagement with crewed deep-space exploration.

See NASA →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 Sets New Standards for Immersive 360 FPV Flying

Equipped with DJI’s flagship 1-inch imaging, O4+ video transmission, and omnidirectional obstacle sensing, creators can now...

DJI's omnidirectional obstacle avoidance on the Avata 360 significantly reduces pilot workload in 360-degree FPV operations, enabling safer exploration of complex environments previously requiring manual collision management.

See sUAS News →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Sustainable aviation requires integrated optimization across airline, airport, and flight operations rather than isolated technological fixes.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Southwest Airlines Transforms Austin-Bergstrom Airport, Unleashing A New Era For Travelers And Crew

The carrier has scheduled almost 3,500 departures from the Texan facility this month alone.

Southwest's massive capacity expansion at Austin signals a shift toward secondary markets as primary hubs face congestion constraints.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Pentagon eyes redirecting Ukraine’s Patriot missiles to Middle East

The Pentagon is considering whether to redirect air defense interceptor missiles originally earmarked for Ukraine to the Middle…...

Strategic reallocation of Patriot inventory between theaters signals potential constraints on U.S. air defense missile production capacity amid simultaneous global conflicts.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Virgin Australia named world’s best cabin crew for eighth year running

Virgin Australia has been named World’s Best Cabin Crew for the eighth consecutive year in the AirlineRatings.com World’s…...

Consistent recognition of cabin crew excellence signals that sustained investment in service training and employee culture drives competitive differentiation in a commoditized market segment.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport Remains North America's Busiest By Total Passengers

With over 100 million passengers handled in 2025, ATL continues to dominate global rankings for the world's busiest airport.

ATL's 100+ million annual passengers create substantial operational demands requiring sophisticated ground infrastructure, slot management, and workforce scaling that challenge even the most advanced airport systems.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA’s Hubble Detects First-Ever Spin Reversal of Tiny Comet

Astronomers using NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope have found evidence that the spinning of a small comet slowed and then reversed its direction of rotation, offering a dramatic example of how volatile activity can affect the spin and physical evolution of small bodies in the solar system. This is the...

Comet spin reversals driven by volatile outgassing provide insights into thermal dynamics that could inform spacecraft design for missions near small celestial bodies.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

Hughes targets sovereign satcom demand with network control software

Company rolls out AI-enabled modem as militaries shift to hybrid networks...

Hughes' AI-enabled modem addresses militaries' transition to hybrid satellite networks, positioning the company to capture growing sovereign satcom demand through intelligent network management.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Russian Geran Drone Shot Down by Rifle-Armed Ukrainian Yak-52 Crew

A first-person point-of-view video shows the crew member in the rear seat of a Ukrainian Yak-52 shooting with a rifle at a Russian Geran drone, which later detonates in a powerful explosion. A video that emerged on Mar. 24, 2026, shows a Russian Geran-type One-Way Attack (OWA) drone exploding...

Ukrainian pilots are adapting vintage aircraft with improvised anti-drone tactics, demonstrating how legacy platforms can address modern threats when conventional air defenses are unavailable.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 26, 2026

$147,000 Damage: Military KC-46A Tanker Rips Up 25 Foot Section Of Airport Tarmac

The continuous high temperature and pressure from the engine blast affected the integrity of the parking ramp, resulting in this damage.

Engine blast damage to airport infrastructure reveals critical vulnerability in KC-46A parking procedures and potential need for revised maintenance area requirements.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Here’s How Much Larger The Boeing 777-300ER’s Windows Are Compared To The Airbus A350

Did you know that the older Triple-Seven has larger windows than a 21st-century clean-sheet carbon-composite widebody?

Larger windows increase cabin pressure differential loads, explaining why newer aircraft like the A350 prioritize smaller windows despite passenger preference for natural light.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Mar 26, 2026

AirAsia Philippines risks losing licenses over $14.5 million unpaid fees

The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) has issued a final demand letter to AirAsia Philippines for…...

Regulatory enforcement against unpaid aviation fees signals stricter compliance oversight that could force operational shutdowns for carriers with financial troubles.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Volotea closes smaller than anticipated funding round following strong results

Regional low-cost carrier Volotea has completed its latest capital increase round. The airline announced in September 2024 that…...

Volotea's decision to close a smaller-than-planned funding round despite strong operational results suggests investor confidence may be shifting toward profitability over growth expansion in the regional carrier segment.
